title: Qlog Contained CBOR Serialization Verification
scope: Verify the first contained qlog CBOR serializer slice, including its sibling-package boundary, retained-model reuse, stream-first binary writer boundary, contained artifact identity policy, and preservation of contained qlog semantics and extension members.
verification_method: Specification conformance checks, requirement-homed unit tests, structural assertions over contained CBOR artifacts, and regression comparison against the existing JSON serializer surfaces.
preconditions: The qlog SpecTrace artifacts validate successfully under the repository's JSON validation script.
The contained qlog CBOR serializer implementation exists in a sibling package.
The qlog artifact metadata policy for the CBOR surface is explicit enough to verify.
procedure: Run scripts/Validate-SpecTraceJson.ps1 over the qlog canonical artifacts.
Run requirement-homed tests for the `REQ-QLOG-CBOR` family.
Confirm that the CBOR serializer lives in the sibling package and that its Stream entry points emit binary output without going through intermediate JSON text.
Verify that contained file metadata, trace metadata, common fields, schema URIs, extension members, and the selected artifact identity policy survive the CBOR mapping.
Run the existing JSON serializer and qlog model regression tests to confirm the CBOR slice did not reshape the retained model.
expected_result: The first contained qlog CBOR serializer is verified as a sibling package over the retained model, preserves the same contained qlog semantics as the JSON baseline, uses the selected `application/cbor` and `.qlog.cbor` artifact policy, and adds stream-first binary output without widening the unstable capture and sink contracts.
